background: A particular random sample of $n$ observations can be used to calculate a sample proportion. The count of successes in the sample will vary according to a probability distribution. When the sample is large, the number of successes will vary according to a Normal distribution. At the same time, the sampling distribution of proportion $\hat{p}$ in large samples will be Normally distributed.

task.query: The count of successes in the sample will vary according to a (a) $\_\_\_\_$ probability distribution with parameters $n$ and (b) $\_\_\_\_$. What is the probability distribution and its parameters?

When the sample is large, the number of successes will vary according to a Normal distribution with $\mu=(c)$ $\_\_\_\_$ and $\sigma=(d)$ $\_\_\_\_$. What are the mean and standard deviation of this Normal distribution?

The sampling distribution of proportion $\hat{p}$ in large samples will be Normally distributed with mean $p$ and standard deviation equal to (e) $\_\_\_\_$. What is the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$?

meta info.caption: Fill in the blanks.
meta info.section: 8.3 SAMPLING BEHAVIOR OF A COUNT AND PROPORTION
